file.content:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

content.text: My feature only touches `outlines/types/__init__.py` — a single, small addition after the `isbn` definition. Let me coordinate with agent2 now.

input.command: coop-send agent2 --type "PROPOSE" --my_files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "Adding new 'ssn' Regex type right after the isbn definition in this file. Small, localized addition - happy to adjust if you also need this file."

coop-send agent2 --type "ACCEPT" --my_files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --your_files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "Both additive to the same file - fine as long as we don't overlap lines. I'll insert 'ssn' immediately after the isbn definition (current end of file, ~line 70). Please append your hash_sha256 definition after my ssn block (i.e., at the very end of the file) to avoid a merge collision. Will send DONE once applied."

"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

lines: +# Matches a US Social Security Number in the standard XXX-XX-XXXX format.

content.text: Now let me verify this works correctly.

input.command:
python -c "
from outlines.types import ssn
print(ssn)
print(ssn.pattern)
import re
print(bool(re.fullmatch(ssn.pattern, '123-45-6789')))
print(bool(re.fullmatch(ssn.pattern, '12-345-6789')))
"

content.text: The `ssn` type works as expected. Now let me test it with pydantic as in the usage example, and generate the patch.

input.command:

from outlines.types import ssn
from pydantic import BaseModel

class User(BaseModel):
    name: str
    social_security_number: ssn
print('import ok')
"

+++ b/outlines/types/__init__.py
@@ -68,3 +68,6 @@ email = Regex(
 isbn = Regex(
     r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
 )
+
+# Matches a US Social Security Number in the standard XXX-XX-XXXX format.
+ssn = Regex(r"\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}")
